metadata.title: Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products for Hormone Replacement Therapy of Oestrogen Deficiency Symptoms in Postmenopausal Women

rules.rule_text: HRT should use the minimum effective dose for the shortest duration. Risk/benefit appraisal must be undertaken at least annually, and HRT continued only as long as benefit outweighs risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: for the initiation and the continuation of treatment, the minimum effective dose for the shortest duration should be used. And in all cases, a careful appraisal of the risks and benefits should be undertaken at least annually and HRT should only be continued as long as the benefit outweighs the risk (core SmPC for HRT).

rules.rule_text: For transdermal HRT bioequivalence claims, bioequivalence must be determined at steady state. Each type of transdermal form must be compared with a similar, approved system (e.g., matrix vs matrix). AUC and Cmax 90% CI must lie entirely within 80-125%.
rules.verbatim_quote: for transdermal pharmaceutical forms, bioequivalence should be determined at steady state. Each type of transdermal pharmaceutical form is recommended to be compared with a similar, approved system (e.g., matrix vs matrix). The following criteria should be fulfilled on the basis of two-sided 90% confidence intervals (CI) for the ratio of means (test/reference). AUC, Cmax: CI lies entirely within the range 80-125%
rules.exceptions: Bioequivalence data not needed for all strengths if dose-proportionality is demonstrated

rules.rule_text: The daily dose for transdermal HRT must be based on pharmacokinetic bioequivalence data. Correction for 'amount released from patch' is not accepted.
rules.verbatim_quote: The daily dose stated for a transdermal system should be based on pharmacokinetic bioequivalence data. Hence, correction for 'amount released from patch' is not accepted.

rules.rule_text: Only moderate to severe hot flushes are to be treated by HRT. The primary efficacy endpoint is frequency of moderate to severe hot flushes. Enrolled subjects must have at least 5 moderate to severe hot flushes per day at baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: Only moderate to severe and severe hot flushes are to be treated by HRT. The proposed primary endpoint for efficacy trials is the frequency of moderate to severe hot flushes. Enrolled subjects should have a defined minimum of hot flushes per day at baseline to justify a need for treatment (at least 5 moderate to severe hot flushes).

rules.rule_text: Placebo-controlled studies are considered sufficient to demonstrate efficacy of an oestrogenic HRT product. Treatment duration of 3 months is generally recommended for symptom efficacy evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Placebo-controlled studies are considered sufficient to demonstrate efficacy of an oestrogenic product. A duration of treatment of 3 months is generally recommended for symptom efficacy evaluation.

rules.rule_text: Endometrial safety data are required for any new combination of oestrogen/progestogen (including new administration scheme, new strength, or new progestogen in a fixed combination).
rules.verbatim_quote: For a new combination of oestrogen/progestogen (e.g. new administration scheme or new strength) or a new progestogen in a fixed combination, endometrial data are required.
rules.exceptions: Exception for known progestogen at same route/dose as in known fixed combination where oestrogen exposure is similar or lower

rules.rule_text: Endometrial biopsy is the gold standard method for endometrial safety assessment and cannot be replaced by uterine ultrasound. Biopsies must be assessed according to predefined, generally accepted microscopic criteria.
rules.source_section: 2.1.1 Endometrial biopsy is the gold standard method
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessment of endometrial biopsies should be made according to predefined and generally accepted microscopic criteria... Transvaginal ultrasonography, does not appear to be sufficiently standardised. Therefore, endometrial thickness measured by transvaginal ultrasound is not recommended to replace the biopsy for the evaluation of endometrial hyperplasia.
rules.exceptions: Ultrasound can help when insufficient tissue is obtained; biopsies with insufficient tissue and endometrial thickness <5mm may be considered atrophic

rules.rule_text: Endometrial biopsies must be conducted at baseline, at end of study, and/or at end of treatment (if stopped before end of study, provided >3 months treatment). For sequential combined treatment, biopsies must be obtained at least 10 days after start of progestogen administration. All endometrial tissue must be processed by a central laboratory.
rules.source_section: 2.1.1 Endometrial biopsy is the gold standard method
rules.verbatim_quote: A biopsy should be conducted at baseline, at the end of the study and/or at the end of treatment if it is stopped before the end of study (provided that it is longer than 3 months)... For sequentially combined treatment, it is recommended that biopsies be obtained at a pre-specified time in the treatment cycle-(at least 10 days after the start of the progestogen-administration)... The endometrial tissue obtained by endometrial biopsy at screening, during the conduct of the study, and at the end-of-study should be processed in the same manner by a central laboratory.
rules.exceptions: For continuous combined treatment, biopsy may be performed at any time during the cycle

rules.rule_text: Patients with histologically proven endometrial hyperplasia or cancer must be excluded from enrolment. Patients with uterine polyps are also recommended for exclusion.
rules.source_section: 2.1.1 Endometrial biopsy is the gold standard method
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with histologically proven endometrial hyperplasia or cancer should be excluded from enrolment. Patients with uterine polyps are also recommended to be excluded from enrolment

rules.rule_text: Minimum endometrial safety study duration is one year for new oestrogen/progestogen combinations. For long-acting products (medicated IUDs, implants), the duration must cover the claimed duration of effectiveness.
rules.source_section: 2.1.3 A minimal duration of one year is necessary
rules.verbatim_quote: For a new combination of oestrogen/progestogen, studies of at least 12 month duration are required. For long-acting products (e.g. medicated IUDs, implants) the duration should cover the claimed duration of effectiveness in the prevention of endometrial hyperplasia.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of vaginal bleeding in clinical HRT trials is mandatory for the full 12-month trial duration. Particular attention to bleeding during the first 3 months and the last 3 months (months 10-12) is required.
rules.source_section: 2.1.4 Assessment of vaginal bleeding in clinical HRT trials is mandatory
rules.verbatim_quote: In case of persistent and/or recurrent bleeding/spotting during treatment, the endometrium should be investigated to identify cases of hyperplasia and cancer... The assessment should be done during the whole trial duration (12 months). Particular attention will be drawn to the findings during the first 3 months (months 0 - 3) and the last 3 months (months 10 - 12) (required in the core SmPC).

rules.rule_text: Endometrial biopsies must be assessed by two independent pathologists blinded to treatment and time of biopsy. In case of disagreement on hyperplasia or carcinoma, a third blinded pathologist must make the final determination.
rules.source_section: 2.1.5 Methodological requirements - Assessment of endometrial biopsies
rules.verbatim_quote: Biopsies should be assessed by two independent pathologists blinded to treatment and time of biopsy. In case of disagreement in the interpretation of results (e.g. on hyperplasia or carcinoma diagnosis) between the two pathologists, a third one, also blinded, should be called upon to make the final determination.

rules.rule_text: Endometrial biopsies must be classified into standardised categories: atrophic, proliferative, secretory, hyperplasia without atypia, hyperplasia with atypia, cancer, and others.
rules.verbatim_quote: The endometrial biopsies should be classified, according to standardized criteria into the general classes of atrophic, proliferative, secretory, hyperplasia without atypia, hyperplasia with atypia, cancer and others.

rules.rule_text: For a new HRT, the incidence of endometrial hyperplasia/carcinoma must be statistically less than 2% after one year: the upper limit of a two-sided 95% CI must not exceed 2%. A sample size of 300 patients treated for one year provides >80% statistical power under this assumption.
rules.verbatim_quote: For a new HRT, a reasonable requirement is that the incidence should be statistically less than 2% after one year of treatment, i.e. the upper limit of a two-sided 95% confidence interval of the observed frequency of endometrial events should not exceed 2%... a sample size of 300 patients treated for one year should provide more than 80% statistical power.

rules.rule_text: Endometrial safety data can be pooled across studies only if: the oestrogen and progestogen formulations are the same, doses are the same (except for lower oestrogen doses), duration of treatment is the same, and treatment sequence is the same.
rules.source_section: 2.1.5 Methodological requirements - Pooling of the data
rules.verbatim_quote: Endometrial safety data from different studies can be pooled if: the oestrogen and progestogen formulations are the same, AND the progestogen and oestrogen doses are the same, except for lower oestrogen doses... AND the duration of treatment is the same, AND the treatment sequence is the same.
rules.exceptions: Lower oestrogen doses are acceptable for pooling

rules.rule_text: The incidence rate of endometrial hyperplasia/carcinoma must be calculated as: Number of hyperplasia/carcinoma divided by Number of evaluable biopsies at 1 year. The upper limit of the two-sided 95% CI must be <=2%.
rules.verbatim_quote: The incidence rate of endometrial hyperplasia should be calculated by taking into account the number of evaluable biopsies at 1 year... To conclude to an established endometrial safety, the upper limit of the two-sided 95% CI of the incidence of hyperplasia/carcinoma should be <= 2%.

rules.rule_text: Lipid, glucose homeostasis, and hemostatic variables are of potential interest and must be studied for new oestrogen/progestogen combination products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Lipid, glucose homeostasis and hemostatic variables are of potential interest for new combination of oestrogen and progestogen products.

rules.rule_text: Careful monitoring of venous thromboembolism (deep leg/pelvic venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism) is strongly recommended in HRT trials, as VTE is more frequent in HRT users than non-users.
rules.verbatim_quote: Venous thromboembolism, i.e. deep leg or pelvic venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism, is more frequent in hormone replacement therapy users than in non-users (see core SmPC for HRT). Therefore, a careful monitoring of this kind of events is strongly recommended.

rules.rule_text: Bleeding control data from comparative studies of at least 12 months duration are required. For cyclic/sequential products: percentage with regular withdrawal bleeding, mean duration, timing relative to progestogen phase, breakthrough bleeding/spotting in months 0-3 and 10-12, and amenorrhoea incidence. For continuous combined products: amenorrhoea incidence in months 10-12, and bleeding/spotting in months 0-3 and 10-12.
rules.verbatim_quote: All data should come from comparative studies to make an evaluation possible. The minimum duration of these studies should be at least 12 months, unless justified by the applicant.
rules.exceptions: Duration may be different if justified by applicant

rules.rule_text: Before initiating HRT, a complete personal and family medical history and breast examination including mammography must be performed. Mammography must be performed within a reasonable timeframe before HRT to exclude malignancy. Periodic check-ups during treatment are recommended per nationally accepted screening practices.
rules.verbatim_quote: Before initiating or reinstituting HRT, a complete personal and family medical history should be taken, and a breast examination performed, including mammography. Mammography should be performed within a reasonable time frame before HRT to exclude malignancy. During treatment, periodic check-ups are recommended of a frequency and nature in accordance with nationally accepted screening practices, and adapted to the individual woman.

rules.rule_text: Local tolerance of non-oral HRT formulations must always be assessed. For patches and gels: skin tolerability (irritation, sensitisation, phototoxicity) and adhesivity. For intravaginal rings: vaginal tolerability. For intranasal treatments: local tolerance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Local tolerance of different oestrogen and progestogen formulations (patches, gels, intravaginal rings, intranasal solutions) should always be assessed. The following is recommended to be assessed: For patches and gels: skin tolerability (irritation, sensitisation, phototoxicity), and adhesivity of patches; For intravaginal rings: vaginal tolerability; For intranasal treatments: local tolerance.

rules.rule_text: Hot flush severity must be clinically classified as: mild (sensation of heat without sweating), moderate (sensation of heat with sweating, able to continue activity), or severe (sensation of heat with sweating, causing cessation of activity).
rules.verbatim_quote: The severity of hot flushes is defined clinically as follows: mild: sensation of heat without sweating, moderate: sensation of heat with sweating, able to continue activity, severe: sensation of heat with sweating, causing cessation of activity
